div.head: Mr. Egan to Mr. Blaine.

opener.dateline:
Legation of the United States,
Santiago, January 15, 1892.

div.p: Mr. Egan replies to the telegram of the 13th, and says that the fiscal analyzes at great length Johnson’s and Langan’s sworn testimony, which shows that a policeman fired at close range the rifle which killed Riggin, and throws it out, in its entirety, as conflicting; that the same officer ignores the evidence of the officers and surgeon of the Baltimore and the circumstance of Johnson’s tie and shirt being pierced by a bullet, and arrives at the conclusion that Riggin’s death was caused by a revolver shot.
